I've uploaded a code repository in the directory /testbed. Consider the following PR description:

<pr_description>
formatter: Arrow function body breaks after => when JSDoc type cast is in body
## Input

Found in [sveltejs/svelte](https://github.com/sveltejs/svelte) repo.

Files: `packages/svelte/src/compiler/legacy.js` (20+ instances), `packages/svelte/src/compiler/phases/3-transform/client/visitors/EachBlock.js`, `packages/svelte/src/compiler/phases/3-transform/client/visitors/Program.js`, `packages/svelte/src/compiler/phases/3-transform/server/visitors/Program.js`, `packages/svelte/src/compiler/phases/3-transform/shared/transform-async.js`

Minimal reproduction:

```js
// Example 1: .map() callback
const result = items.map(
  (child) => /** @type {SomeType} */ (visit(child))
);

// Example 2: function argument
const body = transform_body(
  state.analysis.instance_body,
  b.id('$.run'),
  (node) => /** @type {Node} */ (context.visit(node))
);
```

## Config

```jsonc
{
  "useTabs": true,
  "singleQuote": true,
  "trailingComma": "none",
  "printWidth": 100
}
```

## Oxfmt output

```js
// Example 1
const result = items.map((child) =>
	/** @type {SomeType} */ (visit(child))
);

// Example 2
const body = transform_body(state.analysis.instance_body, b.id('$.run'), (node) =>
	/** @type {Node} */ (context.visit(node))
);
```

## Prettier output

Prettier version: `3.8.1`

```js
// Example 1
const result = items.map(
	(child) => /** @type {SomeType} */ (visit(child))
);

// Example 2
const body = transform_body(
	state.analysis.instance_body,
	b.id('$.run'),
	(node) => /** @type {Node} */ (context.visit(node))
);
```

## Additional notes

When an arrow function body starts with a JSDoc inline type cast (`/** @type {X} */`), Prettier breaks after the opening parenthesis and keeps the entire arrow expression on one line, while Oxfmt breaks after `=>` and puts the JSDoc type cast + body on the next line.

This pattern occurs 20+ times in `legacy.js` alone (all with the same `.map((child) => /** @type {X} */ (visit(child)))` pattern), plus several other files. It's the most frequent diff in the Svelte repo.

</pr_description>

    39	#[derive(Clone, Copy)]
    40	pub struct FormatJsArrowFunctionExpression<'a, 'b> {
    41	    arrow: &'b AstNode<'a, ArrowFunctionExpression<'a>>,
    42	    options: FormatJsArrowFunctionExpressionOptions,
    43	}
    44	
    45	#[derive(Default, Clone, Copy)]
    46	pub struct FormatJsArrowFunctionExpressionOptions {
    47	    pub assignment_layout: Option<AssignmentLikeLayout>,
    48	    pub call_argument_layout: Option<GroupedCallArgumentLayout>,
    49	    // Determine whether the signature and body should be cached.
    50	    pub cache_mode: FunctionCacheMode,

    53	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    54	pub enum GroupedCallArgumentLayout {
    55	    /// Group the first call argument.
    56	    GroupedFirstArgument,
    57	
    58	    /// Group the last call argument.
    59	    GroupedLastArgument,
    60	}
    61	
    62	impl GroupedCallArgumentLayout {
    63	    pub fn is_grouped_first(self) -> bool {
    64	        matches!(self, GroupedCallArgumentLayout::GroupedFirstArgument)
    65	    }
    66	
    67	    pub fn is_grouped_last(self) -> bool {
    68	        matches!(self, GroupedCallArgumentLayout::GroupedLastArgument)
    69	    }
    70	}
    71	
    72	#[derive(Default, Debug, Clone, Copy)]
    73	pub enum FunctionCacheMode {
    74	    /// Format the body without caching it or retrieving it from the cache.
    75	    #[default]
    76	    NoCache,
    77	
    78	    /// Cache the body during the next [formatting](Format::fmt).
    79	    Cache,
    80	}
    81	
    82	impl<'a, 'b> FormatJsArrowFunctionExpression<'a, 'b> {
    83	    pub fn new(arrow: &'b AstNode<'a, ArrowFunctionExpression<'a>>) -> Self {
    84	        Self { arrow, options: FormatJsArrowFunctionExpressionOptions::default() }
    85	    }
    86	
    87	    pub fn new_with_options(
    88	        arrow: &'b AstNode<'a, ArrowFunctionExpression<'a>>,
    89	        options: FormatJsArrowFunctionExpressionOptions,
    90	    ) -> Self {
    91	        Self { arrow, options }
    92	    }
    93	
    94	    #[inline]
    95	    pub fn format(&self, f: &mut Formatter<'_, 'a>) {
    96	        let layout = ArrowFunctionLayout::for_arrow(self.arrow, self.options);
    97	
    98	        match layout {
    99	            ArrowFunctionLayout::Chain(chain) => {
   100	                write!(f, chain);
   101	            }
   102	            ArrowFunctionLayout::Single(arrow) => {
   103	                let body = &arrow.body();
   104	
   105	                let formatted_signature = format_with(|f| {
   106	                    write!(
   107	                        f,
   108	                        [
   109	                            format_signature(
   110	                                arrow,
   111	                                self.options.call_argument_layout.is_some(),
   112	                                true,
   113	                                self.options.cache_mode
   114	                            ),
   115	                            space(),
   116	                            "=>"
   117	                        ]
   118	                    );
   119	                });
   120	
   121	                let format_body = FormatMaybeCachedFunctionBody {
   122	                    body,
   123	                    expression: arrow.expression(),
   124	                    mode: self.options.cache_mode,
   125	                };
   126	
   127	                // With arrays, arrow self and objects, they have a natural line breaking strategy:
   128	                // Arrays and objects become blocks:
   129	                //
   130	                //    [
   131	                //      100000,
   132	                //      200000,
   133	                //      300000
   134	                //    ]
   135	                //
   136	                // Arrow self get line broken after the `=>`:
   137	                //
   138	                //  (foo) => (bar) =>
   139	                //     (foo + bar) * (foo + bar)
   140	                //
   141	                // Therefore if our body is an arrow self, array, or object, we
   142	                // do not have a soft line break after the arrow because the body is
   143	                // going to get broken anyways.
   144	                let arrow_expression = arrow.get_expression();
   145	
   146	                if let Some(Expression::SequenceExpression(sequence)) = arrow_expression {
   147	                    return if let Some(format_sequence) =
   148	                        format_sequence_with_leading_comment(sequence.span(), &format_body, f)
   149	                    {
   150	                        write!(f, [group(&format_args!(formatted_signature, format_sequence))]);
   151	                    } else {
   152	                        write!(
   153	                            f,
   154	                            [group(&format_args!(
   155	                                formatted_signature,
   156	                                group(&format_args!(
   157	                                    space(),
   158	                                    token("("),
   159	                                    soft_block_indent(&format_body),
   160	                                    token(")")
   161	                                ))
   162	                            ))]
   163	                        );
   164	                    };
   165	                }
   166	
   167	                write!(f, formatted_signature);
   168	
   169	                let body_has_soft_line_break =
   170	                    arrow_expression.is_none_or(|expression| match expression {
   171	                        Expression::ArrowFunctionExpression(_)
   172	                        | Expression::ArrayExpression(_)
   173	                        | Expression::ObjectExpression(_) => {
   174	                            !f.comments().has_leading_own_line_comment(body.span().start)
   175	                        }
   176	                        Expression::JSXElement(_) | Expression::JSXFragment(_) => true,
   177	                        _ => {
   178	                            is_multiline_template_starting_on_same_line(expression, f.source_text())
   179	                                || is_huggable_html_embed(expression, f)
   180	                        }
   181	                    });
   182	
   183	                if body_has_soft_line_break {
   184	                    write!(f, [space(), format_body]);
   185	                } else {
   186	                    let should_add_parens = arrow.expression && should_add_parens(body);
   187	
   188	                    let is_last_call_arg = matches!(
   189	                        self.options.call_argument_layout,
   190	                        Some(GroupedCallArgumentLayout::GroupedLastArgument)
   191	                    );
   192	
   193	                    let should_add_soft_line = is_last_call_arg
   194	                        // if it's inside a JSXExpression (e.g. an attribute) we should align the expression's closing } with the line with the opening {.
   195	                        || (matches!(self.arrow.parent(), AstNodes::JSXExpressionContainer(container)
   196	                            if !f.context().comments().has_comment_in_range(arrow.span.end, container.span.end)));
   197	
   198	                    write!(
   199	                        f,
   200	                        group(&format_args!(
   201	                            soft_line_indent_or_space(&format_with(|f| {
   202	                                if should_add_parens {
   203	                                    write!(f, if_group_fits_on_line(&"("));
   204	                                }
   205	
   206	                                write!(f, format_body);
   207	
   208	                                if should_add_parens {
   209	                                    write!(f, if_group_fits_on_line(&")"));
   210	                                }
   211	                            })),
   212	                            is_last_call_arg.then_some(&FormatTrailingCommas::All),
   213	                            should_add_soft_line.then_some(soft_line_break())
   214	                        ))
   215	                    );
   216	                }
   217	            }
   218	        }
   219	    }
   220	}
   221	
   222	impl<'a> Format<'a> for FormatJsArrowFunctionExpression<'a, '_> {
   223	    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut Formatter<'_, 'a>) {
   224	        self.format(f);
   225	    }
   226	}
   227	
   228	enum ArrowFunctionLayout<'a, 'b> {
   229	    /// Arrow function with a non-arrow function body
   230	    Single(&'b AstNode<'a, ArrowFunctionExpression<'a>>),
   231	
   232	    /// A chain of at least two arrow functions.
   233	    ///
   234	    /// An arrow function is part of the chain when it is the body of the parent arrow function.
   235	    ///
   236	    /// The idea of arrow chains is that they break after the `=>` token
   237	    ///
   238	    /// ```javascript
   239	    /// const x =
   240	    ///   (a): string =>
   241	    ///   (b) =>
   242	    ///   (c) =>
   243	    ///   (d) =>
   244	    ///   (e) =>
   245	    ///     f;
   246	    /// ```
   247	    Chain(ArrowChain<'a, 'b>),
   248	}
   249	
   250	impl<'a, 'b> ArrowFunctionLayout<'a, 'b> {
   251	    /// Determines the layout for the passed arrow function. See [ArrowFunctionLayout] for a description
   252	    /// of the different layouts.
   253	    fn for_arrow(
   254	        arrow: &'b AstNode<'a, ArrowFunctionExpression<'a>>,
   255	        options: FormatJsArrowFunctionExpressionOptions,
   256	    ) -> ArrowFunctionLayout<'a, 'b> {
   257	        let mut head = None;
   258	        let mut middle = Vec::new();
   259	        let mut current = arrow;
   260	        let mut should_break = false;
   261	        let is_non_grouped_or_grouped_last_argument = matches!(
   262	            options.call_argument_layout,
   263	            None | Some(GroupedCallArgumentLayout::GroupedLastArgument)
   264	        );
   265	
   266	        loop {
   267	            if is_non_grouped_or_grouped_last_argument
   268	                && current.expression()
   269	                && let Some(AstNodes::ExpressionStatement(expr_stmt)) =
   270	                    current.body().statements().first().map(AstNode::<Statement>::as_ast_nodes)
   271	                && let AstNodes::ArrowFunctionExpression(next) =
   272	                    &expr_stmt.expression().as_ast_nodes()
   273	            {
   274	                should_break = should_break || Self::should_break_chain(current);
   275	
   276	                should_break = should_break || Self::should_break_chain(next);
   277	
   278	                if head.is_none() {
   279	                    head = Some(current);
   280	                } else {
   281	                    middle.push(current);
   282	                }
   283	
   284	                current = next;
   285	                continue;
   286	            }
   287	            break match head {
   288	                None => ArrowFunctionLayout::Single(current),
   289	                Some(head) => ArrowFunctionLayout::Chain(ArrowChain {
   290	                    head,
   291	                    middle,
   292	                    tail: current,
   293	                    expand_signatures: should_break,
   294	                    options,
   295	                }),
   296	            };
   297	        }
   298	    }
   299	
   300	    /// Returns a `true` result if the arrow function contains any elements which
   301	    /// should force the chain to break onto multiple lines. This includes any kind
   302	    /// of return type annotation if the function also takes parameters (e.g.,
   303	    /// `(a, b): bool => ...`), any kind of rest/object/array binding parameter
   304	    /// (e.g., `({a, b: foo}) => ...`), and any kind of initializer for a parameter
   305	    /// (e.g., `(a = 2) => ...`).
   306	    ///
   307	    /// The complexity of these expressions limits their legibility when printed
   308	    /// inline, so they force the chain to break to preserve clarity. Any other
   309	    /// cases are considered simple enough to print in a single line.
   310	    fn should_break_chain(arrow: &ArrowFunctionExpression<'a>) -> bool {
   311	        if arrow.type_parameters.is_some() {
   312	            return true;
   313	        }
   314	
   315	        let parameters = &arrow.params;
   316	
   317	        // This matches Prettier, which allows type annotations when
   318	        // grouping arrow expressions, but disallows them when grouping
   319	        // normal function expressions.
   320	        if !has_only_simple_parameters(parameters, true) {
   321	            return true;
   322	        }
   323	
   324	        let has_parameters = !parameters.items.is_empty();
   325	        let has_type_and_parameters = arrow.return_type.is_some() && has_parameters;
   326	        has_type_and_parameters || has_rest_object_or_array_parameter(parameters)
   327	    }
   328	}
   329	
   330	/// Returns `true` for a template that starts on the same line as the previous token and contains a line break.
   331	///
   332	///
   333	/// # Examples
   334	///
   335	/// ```javascript
   336	/// "test" + `
   337	///   some content
   338	/// `;
   339	/// ```
   340	///
   341	/// Returns `true` because the template starts on the same line as the `+` token and its text contains a line break.
   342	///
   343	/// ```javascript
   344	/// "test" + `no line break`
   345	/// ```
   346	///
   347	/// Returns `false` because the template text contains no line break.
   348	///
   349	/// ```javascript
   350	/// "test" +
   351	///     `template
   352	///     with line break`;
   353	/// ```
   354	///
   355	/// Returns `false` because the template isn't on the same line as the '+' token.
   356	pub fn is_multiline_template_starting_on_same_line(
   357	    expression: &Expression,
   358	    source_text: SourceText,
   359	) -> bool {
   360	    let (start, template) = match expression {
   361	        Expression::TemplateLiteral(template) => (template.span.start, template.as_ref()),
   362	        Expression::TaggedTemplateExpression(tagged) => (tagged.span.start, &tagged.quasi),
   363	        _ => return false,
   364	    };
   365	
   366	    template.quasis.iter().any(|quasi| source_text.contains_newline(quasi.span))
   367	        && !source_text.has_newline_before(start)
   368	}
   369	
   370	/// Returns `true` if the expression is an HTML embed template that should be hugged.
   371	///
   372	/// This covers both ``html`...` `` tagged templates and ``/* HTML */ `...` `` comment-annotated templates.
   373	/// It is needed when the source is single-line but HTML formatting introduces line breaks.
   374	/// Without this, `ExpandParent` emitted by the HTML formatter causes `will_break()` to return `true`,
   375	/// expanding the surrounding construct instead of hugging.
   376	///
   377	/// Prettier hugs HTML embed templates when the content has leading AND trailing whitespace,
   378	/// or when `htmlWhitespaceSensitivity` is `"ignore"`.
   379	/// In these cases, the template stays on the same line as the parent construct:
   380	/// - Call arguments: ``foo(html`<div>...</div>`)``
   381	/// - Arrow function body: ``const a = (b) => html`<div>...</div>` ``
   382	///
   383	/// When there is no leading+trailing whitespace,
   384	/// `hug: false` is set and the template is expanded (not hugged).
   385	///
   386	/// Prettier achieves this via `label({ embed: true, hug })` + `shouldExpandLastArg`.
   387	/// We replicate it by detecting the same conditions on the expression.
   388	pub fn is_huggable_html_embed(expression: &Expression<'_>, f: &Formatter<'_, '_>) -> bool {
   389	    let template = match expression {
   390	        Expression::TaggedTemplateExpression(tagged) => {
   391	            if !matches!(&tagged.tag, Expression::Identifier(id) if id.name.as_str() == "html") {
   392	                return false;
   393	            }
   394	            // Exclude cases where a line comment between tag and quasi forces a line break
   395	            // e.g., ``html // oops \n`...` ``
   396	            if f.source_text()
   397	                .contains_newline_between(tagged.tag.span().end, tagged.quasi.span.start)
   398	            {
   399	                return false;
   400	            }
   401	            &tagged.quasi
   402	        }
   403	        Expression::TemplateLiteral(template) => {
   404	            // Check for `/* HTML */` leading comment
   405	            let comments = f.comments().comments_before(template.span.start);
   406	            if !comments.last().is_some_and(|comment| {
   407	                comment.is_block() && f.source_text().text_for(&comment.content_span()) == " HTML "
   408	            }) {
   409	                return false;
   410	            }
   411	            template.as_ref()
   412	        }
   413	        _ => return false,
   414	    };
   415	
   416	    // Always hug when htmlWhitespaceSensitivity is "ignore"
   417	    if f.options().html_whitespace_sensitivity_ignore {
   418	        return true;
   419	    }
   420	
   421	    // Hug when the cooked content has both leading and trailing whitespace
   422	    let has_leading_ws = template
   423	        .quasis
   424	        .first()
   425	        .and_then(|q| q.value.cooked.as_ref())
   426	        .is_so<response clipped><NOTE>To save on context only part of this file has been shown to you. You should retry this tool after you have searched inside the file with `grep -n` in order to find the line numbers of what you are looking for.</NOTE>

     2	
     3	use oxc_ast::ast::*;
     4	use oxc_span::GetSpan;
     5	
     6	use crate::{
     7	    ast_nodes::AstNode,
     8	    formatter::{Formatter, TailwindContextEntry, prelude::*, trivia::FormatTrailingComments},
     9	    print::arrow_function_expression::is_multiline_template_starting_on_same_line,
    10	    utils::{
    11	        call_expression::is_test_call_expression,
    12	        format_node_without_trailing_comments::FormatNodeWithoutTrailingComments,
    13	        member_chain::MemberChain, tailwindcss::is_tailwind_function_call,
    14	    },
    15	    write,
    16	};
    17	use arguments::is_simple_module_import;
    18	
    19	use super::FormatWrite;
    20	
    21	impl<'a> FormatWrite<'a> for AstNode<'a, CallExpression<'a>> {
    22	    fn write(&self, f: &mut Formatter<'_, 'a>) {
    23	        let callee = self.callee();
    24	        let type_arguments = self.type_arguments();
    25	        let arguments = self.arguments();
    26	        let optional = self.optional();
    27	
    28	        // Check if this is a Tailwind function call (e.g., clsx, cn, tw)
    29	        let is_tailwind_call = f
    30	            .options()
    31	            .sort_tailwindcss
    32	            .as_ref()
    33	            .is_some_and(|opts| is_tailwind_function_call(&self.callee, opts));
    34	
    35	        // For nested non-Tailwind calls inside a Tailwind context, disable sorting
    36	        // to prevent sorting strings inside the nested call's arguments.
    37	        // (e.g., `classNames("a", x.includes("\n") ? "b" : "c")` - don't sort "\n")
    38	        let was_disabled =
    39	            if !is_tailwind_call && let Some(ctx) = f.context_mut().tailwind_context_mut() {
    40	                let was = ctx.disabled;
    41	                ctx.disabled = true;
    42	                Some(was)
    43	            } else {
    44	                None
    45	            };
    46	
    47	        let is_template_literal_single_arg = arguments.len() == 1
    48	            && arguments.first().unwrap().as_expression().is_some_and(|expr| {
    49	                is_multiline_template_starting_on_same_line(expr, f.source_text())
    50	            });
    51	
    52	        if !is_template_literal_single_arg
    53	            && matches!(
    54	                callee.as_ref(),
    55	                Expression::StaticMemberExpression(_) | Expression::ComputedMemberExpression(_)
    56	            )
    57	            && !is_simple_module_import(self.arguments(), f.comments())
    58	            && !is_test_call_expression(self)
    59	        {
    60	            MemberChain::from_call_expression(self, f).fmt(f);
    61	        } else {
    62	            let format_inner = format_with(|f| {
    63	                // Preserve trailing comments of the callee in the following cases:
    64	                // `call /**/()`
    65	                // `call /**/<T>()`
    66	                if self.type_arguments.is_some() {
    67	                    write!(f, [callee]);
    68	                } else {
    69	                    write!(f, [FormatNodeWithoutTrailingComments(callee)]);
    70	
    71	                    let character = if self.optional {
    72	                        // For optional calls with arguments, preserve trailing comments
    73	                        // between the `callee` and `?.` operator.
    74	                        // `alert/* comment */?.('value')` → `alert /* comment */?.("value");`
    75	                        Some(b'?')
    76	                    } else if self.arguments.is_empty() {
    77	                        // For empty argument calls, preserve trailing comments between
    78	                        // the `callee` and `()`.
    79	                        // `call/**/()` → `call /**/();`
    80	                        Some(b'(')
    81	                    } else {
    82	                        None
    83	                    };
    84	                    if let Some(character) = character {
    85	                        let callee_trailing_comments = f
    86	                            .context()
    87	                            .comments()
    88	                            .comments_before_character(self.callee.span().end, character);
    89	                        write!(f, FormatTrailingComments::Comments(callee_trailing_comments));
    90	                    }
    91	                }
    92	                write!(f, [optional.then_some("?."), type_arguments]);
    93	
    94	                // If this IS a Tailwind function call, push the Tailwind context
    95	                let tailwind_ctx_to_push = if is_tailwind_call {
    96	                    f.options()
    97	                        .sort_tailwindcss
    98	                        .as_ref()
    99	                        .map(|opts| TailwindContextEntry::new(opts.preserve_whitespace))
   100	                } else {
   101	                    None
   102	                };
   103	
   104	                // Push Tailwind context before formatting arguments
   105	                if let Some(ctx) = tailwind_ctx_to_push {
   106	                    f.context_mut().push_tailwind_context(ctx);
   107	                }
   108	
   109	                write!(f, arguments);
   110	
   111	                // Pop Tailwind context after formatting
   112	                if tailwind_ctx_to_push.is_some() {
   113	                    f.context_mut().pop_tailwind_context();
   114	                }
   115	            });
   116	            if matches!(callee.as_ref(), Expression::CallExpression(_)) {
   117	                write!(f, [group(&format_inner)]);
   118	            } else {
   119	                write!(f, [format_inner]);
   120	            }
   121	        }
   122	
   123	        // Restore the previous disabled state
   124	        if let Some(was) = was_disabled
   125	            && let Some(ctx) = f.context_mut().tailwind_context_mut()
   126	        {
   127	            ctx.disabled = was;
   128	        }
   129	    }
   130	}
   131	
   132	impl<'a> FormatWrite<'a> for AstNode<'a, NewExpression<'a>> {
   133	    fn write(&self, f: &mut Formatter<'_, 'a>) {
   134	        write!(f, ["new", space(), self.callee(), self.type_arguments(), self.arguments()]);
   135	    }
   136	}
   137	
   138	impl<'a> FormatWrite<'a> for AstNode<'a, ImportExpression<'a>> {
   139	    fn write(&self, f: &mut Formatter<'_, 'a>) {
   140	        write!(f, ["import"]);
   141	        if let Some(phase) = &self.phase() {
   142	            write!(f, [".", phase.as_str()]);
   143	        }
   144	
   145	        // Use the same logic as CallExpression arguments formatting
   146	        write!(f, self.to_arguments());
   147	    }
   148	}

     1	use oxc_allocator::Vec as ArenaVec;
     2	use oxc_ast::ast::*;
     3	use oxc_span::GetSpan;
     4	
     5	use crate::{
     6	    Buffer, Format, FormatTrailingCommas, TrailingSeparator,
     7	    ast_nodes::{AstNode, AstNodes},
     8	    format_args,
     9	    formatter::{
    10	        Comments, FormatElement, Formatter, SourceText, VecBuffer,
    11	        buffer::RemoveSoftLinesBuffer,
    12	        format_element,
    13	        prelude::{
    14	            FormatElements, Tag, empty_line, expand_parent, format_once, format_with, group,
    15	            soft_block_indent, soft_line_break_or_space, space,
    16	        },
    17	        trivia::format_dangling_comments,
    18	    },
    19	    print::{
    20	        FormatFunctionOptions, FormatJsArrowFunctionExpression,
    21	        FormatJsArrowFunctionExpressionOptions,
    22	        array_element_list::can_concisely_print_array_list,
    23	        arrow_function_expression::{
    24	            FunctionCacheMode, GroupedCallArgumentLayout, is_huggable_html_embed,
    25	            is_multiline_template_starting_on_same_line,
    26	        },
    27	        function::FormatFunction,
    28	        parameters::has_only_simple_parameters,
    29	    },
    30	    utils::{
    31	        call_expression::is_test_call_expression, is_long_curried_call,
    32	        member_chain::simple_argument::SimpleArgument,
    33	    },
    34	    write,
    35	};
    36	
    37	impl<'a> Format<'a> for AstNode<'a, ArenaVec<'a, Argument<'a>>> {
    38	    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut Formatter<'_, 'a>) {
    39	        let l_paren_token = "(";
    40	        let r_paren_token = ")";
    41	
    42	        let arguments = self.as_ref();
    43	
    44	        if self.is_empty() {
    45	            return write!(
    46	                f,
    47	                [
    48	                    l_paren_token,
    49	                    // `call/* comment1 */(/* comment2 */)` Both comments are dangling comments.
    50	                    format_dangling_comments(self.parent().span()).with_soft_block_indent(),
    51	                    r_paren_token
    52	                ]
    53	            );
    54	        }
    55	
    56	        let is_simple_module_import = is_simple_module_import(self, f.comments());
    57	
    58	        let call_expression =
    59	            if !is_simple_module_import && let AstNodes::CallExpression(call) = self.parent() {
    60	                Some(call)
    61	            } else {
    62	                None
    63	            };
    64	
    65	        if is_simple_module_import
    66	            || call_expression.is_some_and(|call| {
    67	                is_commonjs_or_amd_call(self, call, f)
    68	                    || ((self.len() != 2
    69	                        || matches!(
    70	                            arguments.first(),
    71	                            Some(
    72	                                Argument::StringLiteral(_)
    73	                                    | Argument::TemplateLiteral(_)
    74	                                    | Argument::TaggedTemplateExpression(_)
    75	                            )
    76	                        ))
    77	                        && is_test_call_expression(call))
    78	            })
    79	            || is_multiline_template_only_args(self, f.source_text())
    80	            || is_graphql_call_with_single_template_arg(self, call_expression)
    81	            || is_huggable_html_embed_single_arg(self, f)
    82	            || is_react_hook_with_deps_array(self, f.comments())
    83	        {
    84	            return write!(
    85	                f,
    86	                [
    87	                    l_paren_token,
    88	                    format_with(|f| {
    89	                        f.join_with(space()).entries_with_trailing_separator(
    90	                            self.iter(),
    91	                            ",",
    92	                            TrailingSeparator::Omit,
    93	                        );
    94	                    }),
    95	                    r_paren_token
    96	                ]
    97	            );
    98	        }
    99	
   100	        // Check if there's an empty line (2+ newlines) between any consecutive arguments.
   101	        // This is used to preserve intentional blank lines in the original source.
   102	        let has_empty_line = arguments.windows(2).any(|window| {
   103	            let (cur_arg, next_arg) = (&window[0], &window[1]);
   104	
   105	            // Count newlines between arguments, short-circuiting at 2 for performance
   106	            // Check if there are at least two newlines between arguments
   107	            f.source_text()
   108	                .bytes_range(cur_arg.span().end, next_arg.span().start)
   109	                .iter()
   110	                .filter(|&&b| b == b'\n')
   111	                .nth(1)
   112	                .is_some()
   113	        });
   114	
   115	        if has_empty_line
   116	            || (!matches!(self.grand_parent(), AstNodes::Decorator(_))
   117	                && is_function_composition_args(self))
   118	        {
   119	            return format_all_args_broken_out(self, true, f);
   120	        }
   121	
   122	        if let Some(group_layout) = arguments_grouped_layout(self, f) {
   123	            write_grouped_arguments(self, group_layout, f);
   124	        } else if call_expression.is_some_and(|call| is_long_curried_call(call)) {
   125	            let trailing_operator = FormatTrailingCommas::All.trailing_separator(f.options());
   126	            write!(
   127	                f,
   128	                [
   129	                    l_paren_token,
   130	                    soft_block_indent(&format_with(|f| {
   131	                        f.join_with(soft_line_break_or_space()).entries_with_trailing_separator(
   132	                            self.iter(),
   133	                            ",",
   134	                            trailing_operator,
   135	                        );
   136	                    })),
   137	                    r_paren_token,
   138	                ]
   139	            );
   140	        } else {
   141	            let interned = f.intern(&format_args!(
   142	                "(",
   143	                soft_block_indent(&format_with(move |f| {
   144	                    let separator = format_with(|f| write!(f, [",", soft_line_break_or_space()]));
   145	                    f.join_with(&separator).entries(self.iter());
   146	                    write!(
   147	                        f,
   148	                        [(!matches!(self.parent(), AstNodes::ImportExpression(_)))
   149	                            .then_some(FormatTrailingCommas::All)]
   150	                    );
   151	                })),
   152	                ")",
   153	            ));
   154	
   155	            if let Some(element) = interned {
   156	                let should_expand = element.will_break();
   157	                write!(
   158	                    f,
   159	                    group(&format_once(|f| {
   160	                        f.write_element(element);
   161	                    }))
   162	                    .should_expand(should_expand)
   163	                );
   164	            }
   165	        }
   166	    }
   167	}
   168	
   169	/// Tests if a call has multiple anonymous function like (arrow or function expression) arguments.
   170	///
   171	/// ## Examples
   172	///
   173	/// ```javascript
   174	/// compose(sortBy(x => x), flatten, map(x => [x, x*2]));
   175	/// ```
   176	pub fn is_function_composition_args(args: &[Argument<'_>]) -> bool {
   177	    if args.len() <= 1 {
   178	        return false;
   179	    }
   180	
   181	    let mut has_seen_function_like = false;
   182	    let is_call_expression_with_arrow_or_function = |call: &CallExpression| {
   183	        call.arguments.iter().any(|arg| {
   184	            matches!(arg, Argument::FunctionExpression(_) | Argument::ArrowFunctionExpression(_))
   185	        })
   186	    };
   187	
   188	    for arg in args {
   189	        match arg {
   190	            Argument::FunctionExpression(_) | Argument::ArrowFunctionExpression(_) => {
   191	                if has_seen_function_like {
   192	                    return true;
   193	                }
   194	                has_seen_function_like = true;
   195	            }
   196	            Argument::ChainExpression(chain) => {
   197	                return if let ChainElement::CallExpression(call) = &chain.expression {
   198	                    is_call_expression_with_arrow_or_function(call)
   199	                } else {
   200	                    false
   201	                };
   202	            }
   203	            Argument::CallExpression(call) => {
   204	                if is_call_expression_with_arrow_or_function(call) {
   205	                    return true;
   206	                }
   207	            }
   208	            _ => {}
   209	        }
   210	    }
   211	
   212	    false
   213	}
   214	
   215	fn format_all_elements_broken_out<'a, 'b>(
   216	    node: &'b AstNode<'a, ArenaVec<'a, Argument<'a>>>,
   217	    elements: impl Iterator<Item = (Option<FormatElement<'a>>, usize)>,
   218	    expand: bool,
   219	    mut buffer: impl Buffer<'a>,
   220	) {
   221	    write!(
   222	        buffer,
   223	        [group(&format_args!(
   224	            "(",
   225	            soft_block_indent(&format_once(move |f| {
   226	                for (index, (element, lines_before)) in elements.into_iter().enumerate() {
   227	                    if let Some(element) = element {
   228	                        if index > 0 {
   229	                            match lines_before {
   230	                                0 | 1 => write!(f, [soft_line_break_or_space()]),
   231	                                _ => write!(f, [empty_line()]),
   232	                            }
   233	                        }
   234	
   235	                        f.write_element(element);
   236	                    }
   237	                }
   238	
   239	                write!(
   240	                    f,
   241	                    [(!matches!(node.parent(), AstNodes::ImportExpression(_)))
   242	                        .then_some(FormatTrailingCommas::All)]
   243	                );
   244	            })),
   245	            ")",
   246	        ))
   247	        .should_expand(expand)]
   248	    );
   249	}
   250	
   251	fn format_all_args_broken_out<'a, 'b>(
   252	    node: &'b AstNode<'a, ArenaVec<'a, Argument<'a>>>,
   253	    expand: bool,
   254	    mut buffer: impl Buffer<'a>,
   255	) {
   256	    let last_index = node.len() - 1;
   257	    write!(
   258	        buffer,
   259	        [group(&format_args!(
   260	            "(",
   261	            soft_block_indent(&format_with(move |f| {
   262	                for (index, argument) in node.iter().enumerate() {
   263	                    if index > 0 {
   264	                        match f.source_text().get_lines_before(argument.span(), f.comments()) {
   265	                            0 | 1 => write!(f, [soft_line_break_or_space()]),
   266	                            _ => write!(f, [empty_line()]),
   267	                        }
   268	                    }
   269	
   270	                    write!(f, [argument, (index != last_index).then_some(",")]);
   271	                }
   272	
   273	                write!(
   274	                    f,
   275	                    [(!matches!(node.parent(), AstNodes::ImportExpression(_)))
   276	                        .then_some(FormatTrailingCommas::All)]
   277	                );
   278	            })),
   279	            ")",
   280	        ))
   281	        .should_expand(expand)]
   282	    );
   283	}
   284	
   285	pub fn arguments_grouped_layout(
   286	    args: &[Argument],
   287	    f: &Formatter<'_, '_>,
   288	) -> Option<GroupedCallArgumentLayout> {
   289	    // For exactly 2 arguments, we need to check both grouping strategies.
   290	    // To avoid redundant `can_group_expression_argument` calls, we handle this case specially.
   291	    if args.len() == 2 {
   292	        let [first, second] = args else { unreachable!("args.len() == 2 guarantees two elements") };
   293	        let second = second.as_expression()?;
   294	
   295	        // `as_expression()` returns `None` for SpreadElement, which is fine since SpreadElement
   296	        // can never match the last arg's type (used for same-type checking in `should_group_last_argument_impl`).
   297	        let first_expr = first.as_expression();
   298	
   299	        if can_group_expression_argument(second, f) {
   300	            // Check if we should group the last argument (second)
   301	            return should_group_last_argument_impl(2, first_expr, second, f)
   302	                .then_some(GroupedCallArgumentLayout::GroupedLastArgument);
   303	        }
   304	
   305	        // Only check first argument grouping if first is an expression (not SpreadElement)
   306	        should_group_first_argument(first_expr?, second, f)
   307	            .then_some(GroupedCallArgumentLayout::GroupedFirstArgument)
   308	    } else {
   309	        // For other cases (not exactly 2 arguments), only check last argument grouping
   310	        should_group_last_argument(args, f)
   311	            .then_some(GroupedCallArgumentLayout::GroupedLastArgument)
   312	    }
   313	}
   314	
   315	/// Checks if the first argument requires grouping
   316	fn should_group_first_argument(
   317	    first: &Expression,
   318	    second: &Expression,
   319	    f: &Formatter<'_, '_>,
   320	) -> bool {
   321	    match first {
   322	        Expression::FunctionExpression(_) => {}
   323	        // Arrow expressions that are a plain expression or are a chain
   324	        // don't get grouped as the first argument, since they'll either
   325	        // fit entirely on the line or break fully. Only a single arrow
   326	        // with a block body can be grouped to collapse the braces.
   327	        Expression::ArrowFunctionExpression(arrow) => {
   328	            if arrow.expression {
   329	                return false;
   330	            }
   331	        }
   332	        _ => return false,
   333	    }
   334	
   335	    if matches!(
   336	        second,
   337	        Expression::ArrowFunctionExpression(_)
   338	            | Expression::FunctionExpression(_)
   339	            | Expression::ConditionalExpression(_)
   340	    ) {
   341	        return false;
   342	    }
   343	
   344	    let first_span = first.span();
   345	
   346	    // Don't group if there are comments around the first argument:
   347	    // - Before the first argument
   348	    // - After the first argument (next non-whitespace is not a comma)
   349	    // - End-of-line comments between the first and second argument
   350	    if f.comments().has_comment_before(first_span.start)
   351	        || !f.source_text().next_non_whitespace_byte_is(first_span.end, b',')
   352	        || f.comments()
   353	            .comments_in_range(first_span.end, second.span().start)
   354	            .iter()
   355	            .any(|c| c.followed_by_newline())
   356	    {
   357	        return false;
   358	    }
   359	
   360	    is_relatively_short_argument(second)
   361	}
   362	
   363	/// Core logic for checking if the last argument should be grouped.
   364	/// Takes the penultimate argument as an Expression for the 2-argument case,
   365	/// or extracts it from the arguments array for other cases.
   366	fn should_group_last_argument_impl(
   367	    args_len: usize,
   368	    penultimate: Option<&Expression>,
   369	    last: &Expression,
   370	    f: &Formatter<'_, '_>,
   371	) -> bool {
   372	    // Check if penultimate and last are the same type (both Object or both Array)
   373	    if let Some(penultimate) = penultimate
   374	        && matches!(
   375	            (penultimate, last),
   376	            (Expression::ObjectExpression(_), Expression::ObjectExpression(_))
   377	                | (Expression::ArrayExpression(_), Expression::ArrayExpression(_))
   378	                | (Expression::TSAsExpression(_), Expression::TSAsExpression(_))
   379	                | (Expression::TSSatisfiesExpression(_), Expression::TSSatisfiesExpression(_))
   380	                | (Expression::ArrowFunctionExpression(_), Expression::ArrowFunctionExpression(_))
   381	                | (Expression::FunctionExpression(_), Expression::FunctionExpression(_))
   382	        )
   383	    {
   384	        return false;
   385	    }
   386	
   387	    let last_span = last.span();
   388	
   389	    // Don't group if there are comments around the last argument
   390	    let has_comment_before_last = if let Some(penultimate) = penultimate {
   391	        // Check for comments between the penultimate and last argument
   392	        f.comments().comments_in_range(penultimate.span().end, last_span.start).last().is_some_and(
   393	            |c| {
   394	                // Exclude end-of-line comments (treated as previous node's comment)
   395	                // and comments followed by a comma
   396	                !c.followed_by_newline()
   397	                    && !f.source_text().next_non_whitespace_byte_is(c.span.end, b',')
   398	            },
   399	        )
   400	    } else {
   401	        f.comments().has_comment_before(last_span.start)
   402	    };
   403	
   404	    if has_comment_before_last
   405	        // Check for comments after the last argument
   406	        || f.comments().comments_after(last_span.end).first().is_some_and(|c|
   407	        !f.source_text().bytes_contain(last_span.end, c.span.start, b')'))
   408	    {
   409	        return false;
   410	    }
   411	
   412	    match last {
   413	        Expression::ArrayExpression(array) if penultimate.is_some() => {
   414	            // Not for `useEffect`
   415	            if args_len == 2 && matches!(penultimate, Some(Expression::ArrowFunctionExpression(_)))
   416	            {
   417	                return false;
   418	            }
   419	
   420	            !can_concisely_print_array_list(array.span, &array.elements, f)
   421	        }
   422	        _ => true,
   423	    }
   424	}
   425	
   426	/// Checks if the last argument should be grouped.
   427	fn should_group_last_argument(args: &[Argument], f: &Formatter<'_, '_>) -> bool {
   428	    let mut iter = args.iter();
   429	    let Some(last) = iter.next_back().unwrap().as_expression() else {
   430	        return false;
   431	    };
   432	
   433	    let penultimate = iter.next_back().and_then(|arg| arg.as_expression());
   434	
   435	    can_group_expression_argument(last, f)
   436	        && should_group_last_argument_impl(args.len(), penultimate, last, f)
   437	}
   438

Here's the result of running `cat -n` on /testbed/crates/oxc_formatter/src/print/as_or_satisfies_expression.rs:
     1	use oxc_ast::ast::*;
     2	use oxc_span::GetSpan;
     3	
     4	use crate::{
     5	    ast_nodes::{AstNode, AstNodes},
     6	    formatter::{Formatter, prelude::*, trivia::FormatTrailingComments},
     7	    print::{FormatNodeWithoutTrailingComments, FormatWrite},
     8	    write,
     9	};
    10	
    11	impl<'a> FormatWrite<'a> for AstNode<'a, TSAsExpression<'a>> {
    12	    fn write(&self, f: &mut Formatter<'_, 'a>) {
    13	        let is_callee_or_object = is_callee_or_object_context(self.span(), self.parent());
    14	        format_as_or_satisfies_expression(
    15	            self.expression(),
    16	            self.type_annotation(),
    17	            is_callee_or_object,
    18	            "as",
    19	            f,
    20	        );
    21	    }
    22	}
    23	
    24	impl<'a> FormatWrite<'a> for AstNode<'a, TSSatisfiesExpression<'a>> {
    25	    fn write(&self, f: &mut Formatter<'_, 'a>) {
    26	        let is_callee_or_object = is_callee_or_object_context(self.span(), self.parent());
    27	        format_as_or_satisfies_expression(
    28	            self.expression(),
    29	            self.type_annotation(),
    30	            is_callee_or_object,
    31	            "satisfies",
    32	            f,
    33	        );
    34	    }
    35	}
    36	
    37	fn format_as_or_satisfies_expression<'a>(
    38	    expression: &AstNode<'a, Expression>,
    39	    type_annotation: &AstNode<'a, TSType>,
    40	    is_callee_or_object: bool,
    41	    operation: &'static str,
    42	    f: &mut Formatter<'_, 'a>,
    43	) {
    44	    let format_inner = format_with(|f| {
    45	        let type_start = type_annotation.span().start;
    46	
    47	        // Check for block comments between expression and type.
    48	        // Prettier's `handleBinaryCastExpressionComment()` handles these specially.
    49	        // https://github.com/prettier/prettier/blob/fdfa6701767f5140a85902ecc9fb6444f5b4e3f8/src/language-js/comments/handle-comments.js#L1131
    50	        // See also https://github.com/prettier/prettier/blob/3.7.3/tests/format/typescript/as/comments/18160.ts
    51	        let comments = f.context().comments().comments_in_range(expression.span().end, type_start);
    52	        let multiline_comment_position = comments.iter().position(|c| c.is_multiline_block());
    53	        let block_comments =
    54	            if let Some(pos) = multiline_comment_position { &comments[..pos] } else { comments };
    55	
    56	        if !comments.is_empty()
    57	            && let AstNodes::TSTypeReference(reference) = type_annotation.as_ast_nodes()
    58	            && reference.type_name.is_const()
    59	        {
    60	            write!(f, [FormatNodeWithoutTrailingComments(expression)]);
    61	            write!(f, [FormatTrailingComments::Comments(block_comments)]);
    62	            write!(f, [space(), token(operation), space(), token("const")]);
    63	        } else if block_comments.is_empty() {
    64	            write!(f, [FormatNodeWithoutTrailingComments(expression)]);
    65	            write!(f, [space(), token(operation), space(), type_annotation]);
    66	        } else {
    67	            write!(f, [expression, space(), token(operation), space(), type_annotation]);
    68	        }
    69	    });
    70	
    71	    if is_callee_or_object {
    72	        write!(f, [group(&soft_block_indent(&format_inner))]);
    73	    } else {
    74	        write!(f, [format_inner]);
    75	    }
    76	}
    77	
    78	fn is_callee_or_object_context(span: Span, parent: &AstNodes<'_>) -> bool {
    79	    match parent {
    80	        // Static member
    81	        AstNodes::StaticMemberExpression(_) => true,
    82	        AstNodes::ComputedMemberExpression(member) => member.object.span() == span,
    83	        // Or CallExpression callee (Not NewExpression, to align with Prettier)
    84	        // https://github.com/prettier/prettier/blob/fdfa6701767f5140a85902ecc9fb6444f5b4e3f8/src/language-js/print/cast-expression.js#L28-L33
    85	        // NOTE: We may revert this if resolved: https://github.com/prettier/prettier/issues/18406
    86	        // _ => parent.is_call_like_callee_span(span),
    87	        AstNodes::CallExpression(call) => call.callee.span() == span,
    88	        _ => false,
    89	    }
    90	}
    91

use oxfmt::cli::{CliRunResult, CliRunner, format_command, init_miette, init_rayon, init_tracing};

// Pure Rust CLI entry point.
// This CLI only supports the basic `Cli` mode.
// For full featured JS CLI entry point, see `run_cli()` exported by `main_napi.rs`.

#[tokio::main]
async fn main() -> CliRunResult {
    // Parse command line arguments from std::env::args()
    let command = format_command().run();

    init_tracing();
    init_miette();
    init_rayon(command.runtime_options.threads);
    CliRunner::new(command).run()
}

Here's the result of running `cat -n` on /testbed/crates/oxc_formatter/tests/fixtures/mod.rs:
     1	use std::{env::current_dir, fs, path::Path};
     2	
     3	use oxc_allocator::Allocator;
     4	use oxc_formatter::{
     5	    ArrowParentheses, BracketSameLine, BracketSpacing, FormatOptions, Formatter, IndentStyle,
     6	    IndentWidth, JsdocOptions, LineEnding, LineWidth, QuoteProperties, QuoteStyle, Semicolons,
     7	    TrailingCommas, get_parse_options,
     8	};
     9	use oxc_parser::Parser;
    10	use oxc_span::SourceType;
    11	
    12	type OptionSet = serde_json::Map<String, serde_json::Value>;
    13	
    14	/// Resolve format options for a test file by walking up the directory tree
    15	fn resolve_options(test_file: &Path) -> Vec<OptionSet> {
    16	    let mut current_dir = test_file.parent();
    17	
    18	    // Walk up the directory tree looking for options.json
    19	    while let Some(dir) = current_dir {
    20	        let options_file = dir.join("options.json");
    21	        if options_file.exists() {
    22	            if let Ok(content) = fs::read_to_string(&options_file)
    23	                && let Ok(option_sets) = serde_json::from_str::<Vec<OptionSet>>(&content)
    24	            {
    25	                return option_sets;
    26	            }
    27	            break;
    28	        }
    29	
    30	        // Stop at fixtures directory
    31	        if dir.ends_with("fixtures") {
    32	            break;
    33	        }
    34	
    35	        current_dir = dir.parent();
    36	    }
    37	
    38	    // Default: single option set with default options (empty map)
    39	    vec![serde_json::Map::new()]
    40	}
    41	
    42	/// Parse JSON options into FormatOptions
    43	fn parse_format_options(json: &OptionSet) -> FormatOptions {
    44	    let mut options = FormatOptions::default();
    45	
    46	    for (key, value) in json {
    47	        match key.as_str() {
    48	            "semi" => {
    49	                if let Some(b) = value.as_bool() {
    50	                    options.semicolons = if b { Semicolons::Always } else { Semicolons::AsNeeded };
    51	                }
    52	            }
    53	            "singleQuote" => {
    54	                if let Some(b) = value.as_bool() {
    55	                    options.quote_style = if b { QuoteStyle::Single } else { QuoteStyle::Double };
    56	                }
    57	            }
    58	            "jsxSingleQuote" => {
    59	                if let Some(b) = value.as_bool() {
    60	                    options.jsx_quote_style =
    61	                        if b { QuoteStyle::Single } else { QuoteStyle::Double };
    62	                }
    63	            }
    64	            "arrowParens" => {
    65	                if let Some(s) = value.as_str() {
    66	                    options.arrow_parentheses = match s {
    67	                        "always" => ArrowParentheses::Always,
    68	                        "avoid" => ArrowParentheses::AsNeeded,
    69	                        _ => options.arrow_parentheses,
    70	                    };
    71	                }
    72	            }
    73	            "trailingComma" => {
    74	                if let Some(s) = value.as_str() {
    75	                    options.trailing_commas = match s {
    76	                        "none" => TrailingCommas::None,
    77	                        "es5" => TrailingCommas::Es5,
    78	                        "all" => TrailingCommas::All,
    79	                        _ => options.trailing_commas,
    80	                    };
    81	                }
    82	            }
    83	            "printWidth" => {
    84	                if let Some(n) = value.as_u64()
    85	                    && let Ok(width) = LineWidth::try_from(u16::try_from(n).unwrap())
    86	                {
    87	                    options.line_width = width;
    88	                }
    89	            }
    90	            "tabWidth" => {
    91	                if let Some(n) = value.as_u64()
    92	                    && let Ok(width) = IndentWidth::try_from(u8::try_from(n).unwrap())
    93	                {
    94	                    options.indent_width = width;
    95	                }
    96	            }
    97	            "useTabs" => {
    98	                if let Some(b) = value.as_bool() {
    99	                    options.indent_style = if b { IndentStyle::Tab } else { IndentStyle::Space };
   100	                }
   101	            }
   102	            "bracketSpacing" => {
   103	                if let Some(b) = value.as_bool() {
   104	                    options.bracket_spacing = BracketSpacing::from(b);
   105	                }
   106	            }
   107	            "bracketSameLine" | "jsxBracketSameLine" => {
   108	                if let Some(b) = value.as_bool() {
   109	                    options.bracket_same_line = BracketSameLine::from(b);
   110	                }
   111	            }
   112	            "endOfLine" => {
   113	                if let Some(s) = value.as_str() {
   114	                    options.line_ending = match s {
   115	                        "lf" => LineEnding::Lf,
   116	                        "crlf" => LineEnding::Crlf,
   117	                        "cr" => LineEnding::Cr,
   118	                        _ => LineEnding::default(),
   119	                    };
   120	                }
   121	            }
   122	            "quoteProps" => {
   123	                if let Some(s) = value.as_str() {
   124	                    options.quote_properties = match s {
   125	                        "as-needed" => QuoteProperties::AsNeeded,
   126	                        "preserve" => QuoteProperties::Preserve,
   127	                        "consistent" => QuoteProperties::Consistent,
   128	                        _ => QuoteProperties::default(),
   129	                    };
   130	                }
   131	            }
   132	            "jsdoc" => {
   133	                if value.is_object() {
   134	                    options.jsdoc = Some(JsdocOptions::default());
   135	                }
   136	            }
   137	            _ => {}
   138	        }
   139	    }
   140	
   141	    options
   142	}
   143	
   144	/// Format options to a readable string for snapshot display
   145	fn format_options_display(json: &OptionSet) -> String {
   146	    if json.is_empty() {
   147	        return "{}".to_string();
   148	    }
   149	
   150	    let mut parts: Vec<_> = json.iter().map(|(k, v)| format!("{k}: {v}")).collect();
   151	    parts.sort();
   152	    format!("{{ {} }}", parts.join(", "))
   153	}
   154	
   155	/// Format a source file with given options
   156	fn format_source(source_text: &str, source_type: SourceType, options: FormatOptions) -> String {
   157	    let allocator = Allocator::default();
   158	    let ret =
   159	        Parser::new(&allocator, source_text, source_type).with_options(get_parse_options()).parse();
   160	    assert!(ret.errors.is_empty());
   161	
   162	    let formatter = Formatter::new(&allocator, options);
   163	    formatter.build(&ret.program)
   164	}
   165	
   166	/// Generate snapshot for a test file
   167	fn generate_snapshot(path: &Path, source_text: &str) -> String {
   168	    let source_type = SourceType::from_path(path).unwrap();
   169	    let option_sets = resolve_options(path);
   170	
   171	    let mut snapshot = String::new();
   172	    snapshot.push_str("==================== Input ====================\n");
   173	    snapshot.push_str(source_text);
   174	    snapshot.push('\n');
   175	
   176	    snapshot.push_str("==================== Output ====================\n");
   177	
   178	    // Test both `printWidth` for Prettier default `80` and our default `100`.
   179	    // If already specified, test that value as well.
   180	    let option_sets = option_sets.into_iter().flat_map(|original_option_json| {
   181	        let mut option_json_w80 = original_option_json.clone();
   182	        option_json_w80.insert("printWidth".to_string(), serde_json::Value::Number(80.into()));
   183	        let mut option_json_default = original_option_json.clone();
   184	        option_json_default.insert(
   185	            "printWidth".to_string(),
   186	            serde_json::Value::Number(LineWidth::default().value().into()),
   187	        );
   188	
   189	        if original_option_json.contains_key("printWidth") {
   190	            vec![original_option_json, option_json_w80, option_json_default]
   191	        } else {
   192	            vec![option_json_w80, option_json_default]
   193	        }
   194	    });
   195	
   196	    for option_json in option_sets {
   197	        let options_line = format_options_display(&option_json);
   198	        let separator = "-".repeat(options_line.len());
   199	
   200	        if !options_line.is_empty() {
   201	            snapshot.push_str(&separator);
   202	            snapshot.push('\n');
   203	            snapshot.push_str(&options_line);
   204	            snapshot.push('\n');
   205	            snapshot.push_str(&separator);
   206	            snapshot.push('\n');
   207	        }
   208	
   209	        let options = parse_format_options(&option_json);
   210	        let formatted = format_source(source_text, source_type, options);
   211	        snapshot.push_str(&formatted);
   212	        snapshot.push('\n');
   213	    }
   214	
   215	    snapshot.push_str("===================== End =====================\n");
   216	
   217	    snapshot
   218	}
   219	
   220	/// Helper function to run a test for a single file
   221	fn test_file(path: &Path) {
   222	    let source_text = fs::read_to_string(path).unwrap();
   223	    let snapshot = generate_snapshot(path, &source_text);
   224	    let snapshot_path = current_dir().unwrap().join(path.parent().unwrap());
   225	    let snapshot_name = path.file_name().unwrap().to_str().unwrap();
   226	
   227	    insta::with_settings!({
   228	        snapshot_path => snapshot_path,
   229	        prepend_module_to_snapshot => false,
   230	        snapshot_suffix => "",
   231	        omit_expression => true,
   232	
   233	    }, {
   234	        insta::assert_snapshot!(snapshot_name, snapshot);
   235	    });
   236	}
   237	
   238	// Include auto-generated test functions from build.rs
   239	include!(concat!(env!("OUT_DIR"), "/generated_tests.rs"));
   240

Now let me look at where the arrow function body formatting decision is made. The key issue is that when an arrow function has a body that is a parenthesized expression (like `(visit(child))`) preceded by a JSDoc comment (`/** @type {SomeType} */`), the formatter breaks after `=>` instead of keeping the whole thing on one line (or breaking after the opening paren).

Looking at the code, when `body_has_soft_line_break` is false, the body is placed after `=>` with a `soft_line_indent_or_space`. When it's true, it uses `space` then body. But the issue is about what happens when it doesn't fit on one line.

The issue is that Prettier breaks after the opening parenthesis and keeps the entire arrow expression on one line, while Oxfmt breaks after `=>` and puts the JSDoc type cast + body on the next line.

So `ParenthesizedExpression` is not preserved - it's essentially just the inner expression. So the `/** @type {SomeType} */ (visit(child))` is parsed as: the leading comment `/** @type {SomeType} */` attached to the parenthesized expression (which becomes just the inner expression `visit(child)`).

The key issue is about how arrow function body is formatted when there's a leading comment on the body expression.

Let me look at `body_has_soft_line_break` more carefully:

```rust
let body_has_soft_line_break =
    arrow_expression.is_none_or(|expression| match expression {
        Expression::ArrowFunctionExpression(_)
        | Expression::ArrayExpression(_)
        | Expression::ObjectExpression(_) => {
            !f.comments().has_leading_own_line_comment(body.span().start)
        }
        Expression::JSXElement(_) | Expression::JSXFragment(_) => true,
        _ => {
            is_multiline_template_starting_on_same_line(expression, f.source_text())
                || is_huggable_html_embed(expression, f)
        }
    });
```

So when the arrow body is an expression that is NOT an ArrowFunction, ArrayExpression, ObjectExpression, JSXElement, JSXFragment, and not a multiline template or huggable HTML embed, `body_has_soft_line_break` is `false`. This means we go to the `else` branch (line 185) which uses `soft_line_indent_or_space`.

But wait, looking at the expected behavior: Prettier puts the arrow function on one line if it fits, but when it doesn't fit (due to print width), it breaks after the opening paren of the call, not after the `=>`. 

Let me think about this more carefully. The PR description says:

**Input:**
```js
const result = items.map(
  (child) => /** @type {SomeType} */ (visit(child))
);
```

**Prettier output (correct):**
```js
const result = items.map(
	(child) => /** @type {SomeType} */ (visit(child))
);
```

**Oxfmt output (incorrect):**
```js
const result = items.map((child) =>
	/** @type {SomeType} */ (visit(child))
);
```

So Prettier breaks after the opening `(` of `items.map(` and keeps the arrow on one line. Oxfmt breaks after the `=>` instead.

The difference seems to be in how the arrow function body with a leading JSDoc comment is handled. The body `/** @type {SomeType} */ (visit(child))` has a leading comment. This comment might be triggering something that makes the formatter think it should break after `=>`.

Let me look at how comments interact with the arrow function body formatting.

Actually, let me look at `body_has_soft_line_break` again. The body expression is most likely a `CallExpression` (the `visit(child)` part). The `/** @type {SomeType} */` is a comment attached to the expression. 

When `body_has_soft_line_break` is `false`, the code goes to:

```rust
write!(
    f,
    group(&format_args!(
        soft_line_indent_or_space(&format_with(|f| {
            if should_add_parens {
                write!(f, if_group_fits_on_line(&"("));
            }

            write!(f, format_body);

            if should_add_parens {
                write!(f, if_group_fits_on_line(&")"));
            }
        })),
        is_last_call_arg.then_some(&FormatTrailingCommas::All),
        should_add_soft_line.then_some(soft_line_break())
    ))
);
```

This uses `soft_line_indent_or_space`. If the content fits on the line, it uses a space; if it doesn't, it breaks with an indent. This means the break happens after `=>`.

But the issue is that the comment `/** @type {SomeType} */` might be causing the content to not fit or might be forcing a break. Let me check how leading comments are handled for the arrow body.

Let me look at `FormatMaybeCachedFunctionBody`

   788	pub struct FormatMaybeCachedFunctionBody<'a, 'b> {
   789	    /// The body to format.
   790	    pub body: &'b AstNode<'a, FunctionBody<'a>>,
   791	
   792	    /// Is the function body an arrow expression? i.e. `() => expr` instead of `() => {}`
   793	    pub expression: bool,
   794	
   795	    /// If the body should be cached or if the formatter should try to retrieve it from the cache.
   796	    pub mode: FunctionCacheMode,
   797	}
   798	
   799	impl<'a> Format<'a> for FormatMaybeCachedFunctionBody<'a, '_> {
   800	    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut Formatter<'_, 'a>) {
   801	        let content = format_with(|f| {
   802	            if self.expression
   803	                && let AstNodes::ExpressionStatement(s) =
   804	                    &self.body.statements().first().unwrap().as_ast_nodes()
   805	            {
   806	                return s.expression().fmt(f);
   807	            }
   808	            self.body.fmt(f);
   809	        });
   810	        FormatContentWithCacheMode::new(self.body.span, content, self.mode).fmt(f);
   811	    }
   812	}
   813	
   814	/// Format a sequence expression in an arrow function body that has a leading comment.
   815	///
   816	/// When an arrow function body is a sequence expression (e.g., `() => (a, b, c)`) and has
   817	/// a leading comment, special formatting is needed to place the comment correctly:
   818	///
   819	/// ```js
   820	/// const f = () =>
   821	///   // comment
   822	///   (a, b, c);
   823	/// ```
   824	///
   825	/// Returns `Some(formatter)` if the sequence has a leading comment, `None` otherwise.
   826	/// When `None`, the caller should use normal formatting with `soft_block_indent`.
   827	///
   828	/// Handles `oxfmt-ignore` by preserving original source text when suppressed.
   829	fn format_sequence_with_leading_comment<'a, 'b>(
   830	    sequence_span: Span,
   831	    format_body: &'b impl Format<'a>,
   832	    f: &Formatter<'_, 'a>,
   833	) -> Option<impl Format<'a> + 'b> {
   834	    if !f.comments().has_comment_before(sequence_span.start) {
   835	        return None;
   836	    }
   837	
   838	    let is_suppressed = f.comments().is_suppressed(sequence_span.start);
   839	
   840	    let format_sequence = format_with(move |f| {
   841	        write!(f, [format_leading_comments(sequence_span), "("]);
   842	        if is_suppressed {
   843	            write!(f, FormatSuppressedNode(sequence_span));
   844	        } else {
   845	            write!(f, format_body);
   846	        }
   847	        write!(f, [")"]);
   848	    });
   849	
   850	    Some(format_with(move |f| {
   851	        write!(f, group(&indent(&format_args!(hard_line_break(), format_sequence))));
   852	    }))
   853	}

2026-05-31 11:38:48,031 - INFO - swea-agent-oxc-project__oxc-20857 - 💭 THOUGHT
Now let me look at the full arrow function expression file to understand the flow better. Let me look at lines 140-220 more carefully:

The key logic is at lines 169-181:
```rust
let body_has_soft_line_break =
    arrow_expression.is_none_or(|expression| match expression {
        Expression::ArrowFunctionExpression(_)
        | Expression::ArrayExpression(_)
        | Expression::ObjectExpression(_) => {
            !f.comments().has_leading_own_line_comment(body.span().start)
        }
        Expression::JSXElement(_) | Expression::JSXFragment(_) => true,
        _ => {
            is_multiline_template_starting_on_same_line(expression, f.source_text())
                || is_huggable_html_embed(expression, f)
        }
    });
```

For a `CallExpression` body (like `visit(child)`), this falls into the `_` case and returns `false` (unless it's a template or HTML embed). So `body_has_soft_line_break` is `false`, and we go to the else branch.

The else branch uses `soft_line_indent_or_space` which means: if it fits on the current line, just put a space and the body; if not, break and indent the body.

So the behavior should be: if `=> /** @type {SomeType} */ (visit(child))` fits on the line, it stays on one line. If it doesn't fit, it breaks after `=>` and indents.

But the PR says Oxfmt breaks after `=>` while Prettier keeps it on one line and breaks after the opening paren of the call. The print width is 100, so `(child) => /** @type {SomeType} */ (visit(child))` is only ~57 characters, which should fit on a line.

So why does Oxfmt break after `=>`? Let me check if the comments force a break somehow.

Let me look at how comments are formatted when they appear before an expression in an arrow function body.

Let me look at the expression formatting for call expressions with leading

🎬 ACTION
cd /testbed && cat > /tmp/test_fmt.rs << 'EOF'
use std::fs;

use oxc_allocator::Allocator;
use oxc_formatter::{Formatter, FormatOptions, IndentStyle, QuoteStyle, TrailingCommas, LineWidth};
use oxc_parser::Parser;
use oxc_span::SourceType;

fn main() {
    let source = "const result = items.map(\n  (child) => /** @type {SomeType} */ (visit(child))\n);\n";
    println!("=== INPUT ===");
    println!("{}", source);
    
    let allocator = Allocator::default();
    let source_type = SourceType::from_path("/tmp/test_arrow.js").unwrap();
    let ret = Parser::new(&allocator, source, source_type).with_options(FormatOptions::default_parse_options()).parse();
    
    if !ret.errors.is_empty() {
        for err in &ret.errors {
            eprintln!("Parse error: {:?}", err);
        }
        return;
    }
    
    let mut options = FormatOptions::default();
    options.indent_style = IndentStyle::Tab;
    options.quote_style = QuoteStyle::Single;
    options.trailing_commas = TrailingCommas::None;
    options.line_width = LineWidth::try_from(100u16).unwrap();
    
    let formatter = Formatter::new(&allocator, options);
    let result = formatter.build(&ret.program);
    println!("=== OUTPUT ===");
    println!("{}", result);
}
EOF

I can see the current behavior. Let me look at the output more clearly:

For printWidth 100:
```
const result = items.map((child) => /** @type {SomeType} */ (visit(child)));
const body = transform_body(state.analysis.instance_body, b.id("$.run"), (node) =>
  /** @type {Node} */ (context.visit(node)),
);
```

So for the first example, it stays on one line (since it fits within 100 chars). For the second example, it breaks after `=>` because it doesn't fit.

But the expected Prettier behavior is:
```
const result = items.map(
	(child) => /** @type {SomeType} */ (visit(child))
);
const body = transform_body(
	state.analysis.instance_body,
	b.id('$.run'),
	(node) => /** @type {Node} */ (context.visit(node))
);
```

So the issue is that when an arrow function with a JSDoc inline type cast body is used as a call argument, Prettier keeps the arrow function on one line and breaks the call arguments instead, while Oxfmt breaks after `=>`.

Now let me understand the call argument grouping logic. The arrow function in the second example is the last argument of `transform_body`. The formatter currently decides to group the last argument (arrow function) and then when the arrow function doesn't fit on one line, it breaks after `=>`.

But the expected behavior is that the call arguments should be expanded (broken out) instead of trying to keep the arrow function on one line.

Let me look at the `can_group_expression_argument` function to understand when an argument is eligible for grouping:



🎬 ACTION

2026-05-31 11:45:16,021 - DEBUG - swea-tools-oxc-project__oxc-20857 - Retrieved state from environment: {'working_dir': '/testbed'}
2026-05-31 11:45:16,026 - INFO - swea-agent-oxc-project__oxc-20857 - 🤖 MODEL INPUT
OBSERVATION:

Here's the result of running `cat -n` on /testbed/crates/oxc_formatter/src/print/call_like_expression/arguments.rs:
   439	/// Check if `ty` is a relatively simple type annotation, allowing a few
   440	/// additional cases through. The simplicity is determined as
   441	/// either being a keyword type or any reference type with no additional type
   442	/// parameters. For example:
   443	/// ```text
   444	///     number          => true
   445	///     unknown         => true
   446	///     HTMLElement     => true
   447	///     string | object => false
   448	///     Foo<string>     => false
   449	/// ```
   450	/// This function also introspects into array and generic types to extract the
   451	/// core type, but only to a limited extent:
   452	/// ```text
   453	///     string[]        => string
   454	///     string[][]      => string
   455	///     string[][][]    => string
   456	///     Foo<string>[][] => string
   457	///     Foo<string[]>[] => string[]
   458	///     Foo<string[][]> => string[][]
   459	/// ```
   460	fn is_simple_ts_type(ty: &TSType<'_>) -> bool {
   461	    // Reach up to two-levels deep into array types:
   462	    //     string[]     => string
   463	    //     string[][]   => string
   464	    //     string[][][] => string[]
   465	    let extracted_array_type = match ty {
   466	        TSType::TSArrayType(array) => match &array.element_type {
   467	            TSType::TSArrayType(inner_array) => &inner_array.element_type,
   468	            element_type => element_type,
   469	        },
   470	        _ => ty,
   471	    };
   472	
   473	    // Then, extract the first type parameter of a Generic as long as it's the
   474	    // only parameter:
   475	    //     Foo<number> => number
   476	    //     Foo<number, string> => Foo<number, string>
   477	    let extracted_generic_type = match &extracted_array_type {
   478	        TSType::TSTypeReference(generic) => {
   479	            if let Some(type_arguments) = &generic.type_arguments {
   480	                let argument_list = &type_arguments.params;
   481	                if argument_list.len() == 1 { &argument_list[0] } else { extracted_array_type }
   482	            } else {
   483	                extracted_array_type
   484	            }
   485	        }
   486	        _ => extracted_array_type,
   487	    };
   488	
   489	    match extracted_generic_type {
   490	        // Any keyword or literal types
   491	        TSType::TSLiteralType(_)
   492	        | TSType::TSTemplateLiteralType(_)
   493	        | TSType::TSThisType(_)
   494	        | TSType::TSBigIntKeyword(_)
   495	        | TSType::TSBooleanKeyword(_)
   496	        | TSType::TSObjectKeyword(_)
   497	        | TSType::TSAnyKeyword(_)
   498	        | TSType::TSNeverKeyword(_)
   499	        | TSType::TSNullKeyword(_)
   500	        | TSType::TSNumberKeyword(_)
   501	        | TSType::TSStringKeyword(_)
   502	        | TSType::TSSymbolKeyword(_)
   503	        | TSType::TSUndefinedKeyword(_)
   504	        | TSType::TSUnknownKeyword(_)
   505	        | TSType::TSVoidKeyword(_) => true,
   506	
   507	        // Any reference with no generic type arguments
   508	        TSType::TSTypeReference(reference) => reference.type_arguments.is_none(),
   509	
   510	        _ => false,
   511	    }
   512	}
   513	
   514	/// Checks if `argument` is "short" enough to be groupable. This aims to be
   515	/// logically similar to Prettier's [`isHopefullyShortCallArgument`](https://github.com/prettier/prettier/blob/093745f0ec429d3db47c1edd823357e0ef24e226/src/language-js/print/call-arguments.js#L279),
   516	fn is_relatively_short_argument(argument: &Expression<'_>) -> bool {
   517	    match argument {
   518	        Expression::BinaryExpression(binary) => {
   519	            SimpleArgument::from(&binary.left).is_simple_with_depth(1)
   520	                && SimpleArgument::from(&binary.right).is_simple_with_depth(1)
